skill.name: elevenlabs-tts
skill.description: Generate ElevenLabs text-to-speech audio through the inference.sh CLI with documented voices, models, output formats, and voice tuning options.
skill.summary: Generate ElevenLabs voiceovers with model, voice, format, and tuning controls through infsh.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, URL, and weak-cryptography patterns. Manual review found the Ruby backtick and weak-cryptography findings to be false positives from Markdown content, while the legitimate infsh CLI and network usage create a medium publication risk that should be disclosed.

medium_findings.title: Broad External CLI Invocation
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill grants Bash access for infsh commands and documents many infsh app run and npx skill-install commands. This is expected for an inference.sh skill, but it can run external commands that may consume credits, contact remote services, or write outputs when users approve execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and repeated command examples confirm external command execution. The commands are narrowly tied to infsh workflows, so the risk is moderate rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Network Service Use
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill routes text-to-speech work through inference.sh and ElevenLabs-related apps, and the documentation includes remote URLs. This is legitimate for text-to-speech generation, but users should expect submitted text and media URLs to leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation links to inference.sh and shows remote app execution through infsh. No credential exfiltration or unrelated endpoint was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The static Ruby/shell backtick findings occur in Markdown code fences, inline command examples, model names, voice names, and table values. These are documentation examples, not Ruby code execution inside the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows Markdown syntax and shell examples rather than Ruby source. There is no Ruby file or backtick expression that would execute at runtime.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The hardcoded URLs point to inference.sh documentation, a hosted image, installation instructions, and a placeholder portrait URL in an example. They do not show hidden callbacks, credential exfiltration, or unrelated network destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in user-facing documentation and examples. They are related to the skill purpose and do not include secrets or encoded payloads.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The flagged lines are the skill description and an output format table header. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithm usage appears at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found descriptive text and audio format documentation only. The static high-severity result does not match the file content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Broad External CLI Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill grants Bash access for infsh commands and documents many infsh app run and npx skill-install commands. This is expected for an inference.sh skill, but it can run external commands that may consume credits, contact remote services, or write outputs when users approve execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and repeated command examples confirm external command execution. The commands are narrowly tied to infsh workflows, so the risk is moderate rather than high.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Network Service Use
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill routes text-to-speech work through inference.sh and ElevenLabs-related apps, and the documentation includes remote URLs. This is legitimate for text-to-speech generation, but users should expect submitted text and media URLs to leave the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documentation links to inference.sh and shows remote app execution through infsh. No credential exfiltration or unrelated endpoint was found.

content.value_statement: Teams need natural narration without building a custom text-to-speech pipeline. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through ElevenLabs voice generation with infsh commands and documented options.
actual_capabilities: Provides infsh commands for ElevenLabs text-to-speech generation.
Lists documented voice IDs and voice styles for selection.
Explains model choices for quality, balanced speed, and low latency.
Shows stability, similarity, style, and speaker boost tuning options.
Documents MP3 and PCM output format options.
Includes multilingual and voice-plus-video workflow examples.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated account.
Sends requested text and media URLs to external services for generation.
Does not verify ElevenLabs account limits, pricing, or licensing terms.
Does not include local audio editing, mastering, or transcript alignment tools.

use_cases.title: Product Demo Narration
use_cases.description: Create clear voiceovers for demos, launch videos, and walkthroughs using a selected voice and output format.

use_cases.title: Course Audio Production
use_cases.description: Generate consistent lesson narration in multiple languages with controlled voice stability and style.

use_cases.title: Prototype Voice Interfaces
use_cases.description: Test IVR prompts, assistant responses, and low-latency speech output before building production integrations.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Voiceover
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a short audio clip.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a short ElevenLabs voiceover for this script using a warm professional voice: [paste script]. Explain the command before running it.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Voice and Model
prompt_templates.scenario: Select settings for quality, latency, and tone.
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend an ElevenLabs voice and model for a product demo script. Prioritize natural delivery, clear pronunciation, and MP3 output.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Delivery Style
prompt_templates.scenario: Adjust stability, similarity, and style settings.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare an infsh ElevenLabs TTS command for this narration. Use a more expressive delivery while keeping the voice consistent across takes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multilingual Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced content production across several languages.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multilingual voiceover workflow for English, Spanish, and French scripts. Include voice choices, model selection, output format, and review steps.

output_examples.input: A 30-second product launch script with a request for a confident British voice.
output_examples.output: A recommended voice, model, and command plan that produces a polished MP3 narration for the launch video.

output_examples.input: A training lesson paragraph that needs warmer delivery and consistent pacing.
output_examples.output: A tuned voiceover setup with stability and style guidance for repeatable lesson audio.

output_examples.input: Three localized welcome messages for a customer support phone system.
output_examples.output: A multilingual generation plan with voice choices and audio format guidance for IVR prompts.

best_practices: Review scripts for private data before sending them to external TTS services.
Start with a short sample clip before generating long narration.
Document voice, model, and tuning settings for repeatable production output.
anti_patterns: Do not paste secrets, customer records, or regulated data into narration text.
Do not assume a voice license covers every commercial use case.
Do not run long generation jobs without checking account limits and expected cost.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It helps generate ElevenLabs text-to-speech audio through the inference.sh CLI using documented voices, models, and output formats.

faq.question: Does it require an external account?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill requires the infsh CLI and login to an account that can access the ElevenLabs TTS app.

faq.question: Can it work in multiple languages?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation describes multilingual support and examples for English, Spanish, and French generation.

faq.question: Can I control the voice style?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents stability, similarity boost, style, and speaker boost parameters.

faq.question: Where does my text go?
faq.answer: Text is sent through inference.sh and the connected TTS service when you run the generation command.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning. The main risks are external command execution and third-party network processing.
